actions.text: ### Key Decisions and Agreements
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Banking discussion postponed to early December** - Dylan pitched RO's fintech banking services (concierge service, live human support, cash back rates), Vrijen expressed interest but wants to revisit when Careerspan has clearer financial picture after shifting to revenue generation. *Why it matters*: Timing aligns with V's need to evaluate margins once new business model is operational; gives V time to assess whether switching from Brex is worth the lift.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Event sponsorship exploration opened** - Vrijen pivoted conversation to RO's event sponsorship program, proposing two event concepts: (1) "blind date for hiring" matching event in December, (2) AI productivity systems workshop showcasing founder operating system built on Zo. *Why it matters*: Creates potential value exchange beyond banking relationship; positions Careerspan's events as founder-focused and aligned with RO's community strategy.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Next steps defined** - Vrijen will send Dylan event details/proposals, Dylan will vet with RO events team and provide feedback on interest level, banking conversation to resume late November/early December. *Why it matters*: Clear path forward with mutual value exploration; event sponsorship could accelerate banking relationship if RO sees value.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: ### Strategic Context
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Dylan represents RO, a fintech banking platform targeting startups with differentiation around: (1) Banking safety (Webster National partnership, larger institutional bank vs. smaller regionals), (2) Concierge service (dedicated relationship manager + account manager + 24/7 live human support), (3) Competitive rates (up to 2% cash back, strong treasury rates). Their go-to-market motion relies heavily on events (5-10/week in NY, SF, Boston) to access founders. They prioritize existing clients for event partnerships.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Vrijen is navigating Careerspan's business model shift from VC-backed career coaching product to bootstrapped decentralized talent network with freemium model for hiring managers. The company recently moved from Mercury to Brex (not V's decision), currently at tail end of runway, focusing on revenue generation starting now. Team of 5 (2 co-founders, 2 devs, 1 designer).
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: V's strategic move: Rather than commit to banking switch immediately, he positioned Careerspan as event organizer with founder audience access - something RO values highly. This creates leverage: if RO sponsors events, that could incentivize V to prioritize banking migration. V also showcased his AI systems expertise (Zo-based founder OS) as additional value-add, signaling he's not just a banking prospect but a potential partner who can deliver valuable touchpoints with target founders.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Pain points surfaced: Banking switches are burdensome (paperwork, operational lift), which is why RO emphasizes their customer architects handling onboarding in 45-minute call. V is at inflection point where margins will matter more as they shift to revenue focus, making rate optimization more relevant than when they were pre-revenue.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window
